channel.title: Combined Politepol Feed
channel.description: Merged feed from multiple Politepol sources

item.title: Did UK minister hold a minute’s silence after Khamenei’s death? The misinformation war amid Iran conflict
20-year-old, mother of two... The tales of first 4 US soldiers killed amid Iran war
Cape of Good Hope, Russian crude: What alternatives does India have if Iran war chokes oil supplies?
Is the US running out of missiles to attack Iran?
Spy dental chip and more.. The many theories on how Mossad tracked Khamenei
How Mojtaba Khamenei, Iran’s new supreme leader, built a £100-million global property empire
US Sutra | The Iran paradox: The strange case of Left-liberal mourning for Tehran
item.description: To eulogise and lament the demise of a barbarous despot and a brutal regime, which trampled on its own citizens and destroyed its own country economically, socially and politically, is sheer folly

item.title: The sad tale of Pakistan: In trying to be too clever by half, hoisted by its own petard as Iran war escalates
item.description: Pakistan’s strategic overreach, driven largely by its desire to outmanoeuvre India, has instead resulted in a strategic squeeze

item.title: Strategic suicide, not strategic depth: Why Pakistan’s ‘jihadi chickens’ in Afghanistan have come home to roost
item.description: As clashes flare along the Durand Line, Islamabad’s long-touted ‘strategic depth’ doctrine collapses under the weight of its own contradictions

item.title: From Achaemenids to Nadir Shah: Debunking the myth of a ‘pacifist’ Persia
item.description: Ancient Iran had a series of powerful empires that left their mark on antiquity. Then as now, imperial power is seldom acquired or advertised through peaceful means, and the more expansionist it is, the more brutal its footprint

item.title: Bangladesh’s International Crimes Tribunal: A syndicate in the guise of justice
item.description: Bribery, manufactured immunity, and selective amnesia have transformed Bangladesh's ICT into a protection racket, laundering the guilt of street-level killers to fuel a partisan purge against the Awami League

item.title: Is it the beginning of the end of the existing Iranian regime?
item.description: The regime appears prepared, security forces remain loyal, and ideological narratives are being activated. Yet structural exhaustion, economic pressure, leadership transition, minority alienation and generational discontent introduce vulnerabilities unseen in earlier crises
